title: Palicourea octocuspis
taxonomy: Palicourea octocuspis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. The genus Palicourea is part of the tribe Palicoureeae and is native to the Americas. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species. The classification of Palicourea octocuspis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within this complex family.

intro: Palicourea octocuspis, also known as Octopus-leaved Palicourea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea octocuspis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Palicourea octocuspis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
